source_note: California/ADA-derived simplified plan-view geometric rules for benchmark evaluation, not a full code compliance review.
scope: Plan-view accessibility repair only. All vertical mounting-height requirements are intentionally excluded from scoring.

turning_space.description: A 60 inch diameter wheelchair turning circle must fit in the usable floor area; diameter alone is not sufficient. In this plan-view benchmark, the room polygon represents the interior finished room boundary. Usable floor is derived from that boundary after a small wall/boundary offset and after subtracting blocked fixtures. The turning circle may overlap toilet clear floor space, but not the bathtub or a lavatory without knee/toe clearance.

door.description: Door must provide a 32 inch clear opening and should not swing into required fixture clearance.

toilet.description: Toilet centerline must be 16 to 18 inches from the adjacent side wall.

grab_bars.description: Only plan-view grab bar presence/orientation/length and toilet-relative placement are in scope; mounting height is excluded. The side-wall grab bar should align with the same adjacent side wall used for the toilet centerline. The rear-wall grab bar should align with the toilet rear wall and cross or closely span the toilet centerline.

lavatory.description: Only plan-view knee/toe clearance width and depth are in scope; rim height, pipe protection, and mirror rules are excluded.

bathtub.description: Bathtub is treated as a protected fixture in minimal-change scoring unless a repair explicitly requires otherwise.

accessories_and_signage.description: Accessories and signage are visible CAD elements but their ADA requirements are vertical or labeling-related and are excluded from first-version scoring.
